justification: All 6 ACs implemented correctly. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED via STATUS_MAP. AC4 uses logger.warn (not error) and returns 'unknown'. AC5 uses toFixed(2)+parseFloat for correct decimal handling (1099→10.99). AC6 uses != null guard covering both null and undefined. 16 tests cover all 6 ACs with explicit criterion-based names; AC3 tested with both inputs; AC4 verifies warn called and verifies no warn for known statuses. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly tests both FAILED and DECLINED inputs
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn, and includes a negative test verifying warn is NOT called for known statuses
AC5 uses toFixed(2)+parseFloat — idiomatic JS/TS approach for 2dp rounding
AC6 uses != null guard which correctly handles both undefined and null
16 tests organized by acceptance criterion with descriptive names
acceptance_criteria_coverage comprehensively maps all 6 ACs with test references

trials.justification: All 6 ACs correctly implemented: AC1/AC2 direct mappings, AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED via STATUS_MAP, AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns 'unknown', AC5 uses (cents/100).toFixed(2) with parseFloat for correct floating-point conversion, AC6 uses != null to cover both null and undefined. Tests cover all 6 ACs with 16 cases across 6 describe blocks. AC3 tested with both inputs. AC4 verifies logger.warn called and tests case-sensitivity. AC5 tested with multiple values including 199→1.99. Logger injected as parameter, no global. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. No external dependencies.
